more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe Exempts Motorbikes From Mandatory ZBC Radio Licence Fees
more.description: Motorbikes, Trailers Excluded From ZBC Radio Licence Payments Confusion surrounding ZBC radio licence payments for motorbike owners has been cleared after the Government confirmed that the category of vehicles is exempt from the charges. Addressing Parliament on Wednesday, Deputy Minister of Information, Publicity and Broadcasting Services Omphile Marupi said motorbikes, trailers, tricycles and some moving [...] The post Zimbabwe Exempts Motorbikes From Mandatory ZBC Radio Licence Fees appeared first on iHarare News .

more.description: A fiery address by opposition-aligned commentator Comrade Knox Chivero has laid bare deep-seated allegations of grand corruption at the heart of Zimbabwe’s ruling establishment The post Comrade Knox accuses Mnangagwa allies of multi-billion dollar looting spree appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: ‘Happy Couple’ Lie Exposed: Kadoma Man Stabs Pregnant Daughter and Wife to Death – Neighbors Reveal Shocking Details
more.description: A City in Mourning: Unpacking the Kadoma Double Murder and Zimbabwe’s Enduring Scourge of Violence KADOMA – The quiet suburb of Victory Park in Kadoma was shattered by an act of unimaginable brutality recently, as 45-year-old Obert Namurokosi allegedly stabbed his wife, Cynthia Namurokosi, and their 23-year-old daughter, Takudzwa Namurokosi, who was nine months pregnant, [...] The post ‘Happy Couple’ Lie Exposed: Kadoma Man Stabs Pregnant Daughter and Wife to Death – Neighbors Reveal Shocking Details first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: 67 farms and the collapse of Zanu PF’s anti-western myth
more.description: Zimbabwe has officially committed to returning 67 farms seized during the Fast Track Land Reform Programme (FTLRP) to their former owners - specifically those protected under Bilateral Investment Protection and Promotion Agreements (BIPPAs). The post 67 farms and the collapse of Zanu PF’s anti-western myth appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: High Court reinstates ZIMURA board after government’s marching orders
more.description: THE High Court has reinstated the Alexio Gwenzi-led Zimbabwe Music Rights Association (ZIMURA) board, just days after the government ordered its dismissal following allegations of corruption and organisational failures. ZIMURA has come under intense scrutiny over allegations of financial mismanagement and maladministration. Musicians have been calling for government intervention, accusing the rights body of underpaying [...] The post High Court reinstates ZIMURA board after government’s marching orders appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Man jailed for raping 80-year-old woman
more.description: A 35-year-old man has been jailed 18 years for raping an 80-year-old woman from Kenzamba, Makonde district, Mashonaland West province. Ricardo Majoni appeared before the Chinhoyi Magistrates Court where he was convicted of rape perpetrated on the octogenarian. In addition to the 18-year sentence, Majoni was slapped with a further one-year jail term for escaping [...] The post Man jailed for raping 80-year-old woman appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Zimbabwe to return 67 foreign-owned farms seized in land grab
more.description: Bloomberg Zimbabwe said it was returning a number of foreign-owned farms that were seized during a violent land grab more than 25 years ago and pay compensation of $146 million. Agriculture Minister Anxious Masuka told lawmakers that the transfer involved 67 properties. Treasury data separately showed the payments would settle claims by property owners from [...] The post Zimbabwe to return 67 foreign-owned farms seized in land grab appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Byo MP demands rescue plan for Zimbabweans in SA
more.description: Bulawayo North Constituency MP, Minenhle Gumede, has called on the Government to urgently coordinate efforts to facilitate the safe and dignified return of Zimbabweans from South Africa, amid renewed fears triggered by a fresh wave of xenophobic tensions.

more.title: BCC cracks down on illegal ECD centres, businesses
more.description: Source: BCC cracks down on illegal ECD centres, businesses — CITEZW Several Early Childhood Development (ECD) centres in Bulawayo are operating without approved building plans and medically examined food handlers, as health inspectors uncovered widespread non-compliance across the city. This emerged in the latest council minutes, which show that last month the Bulawayo City Council [...] The post BCC cracks down on illegal ECD centres, businesses appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Govt finalises arrears, debt clearance roadmap
more.description: Source: Govt finalises arrears, debt clearance roadmap – herald Debra Matabvu Senior Reporter ZIMBABWE has finalised a roadmap for its debt and arrears clearance programme and is targeting early next year to begin repaying outstanding obligations to international creditors. This is a major step towards restoring access to global financing and the plan follows high-level [...] The post Govt finalises arrears, debt clearance roadmap appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zim walks talk on re-engagement

more.title: Harare’s Sally Mugabe Hospital records the highest births in Zimbabwe with 22 000 baby deliveries
more.description: Source: Harare’s Sally Mugabe Hospital records the highest births in Zimbabwe with 22 000 baby deliveries – herald Trust Freddy Zimpapers Correspondent SALLY MUGABE Hospital in Harare continues to dominate the country’s maternal health sector, recording more than 22 000 deliveries last year, the highest number of births at any single institution in Zimbabwe.
